summary: Plan-only remediation assessment for five inventory PRs plus hydrated first-hop context. One PR is security-sensitive and should be routed centrally. No merge is recommended because none has complete merge preflight proof with cleared review state and passed Codex /review. Two narrow contributor PRs have concrete repair paths; broad or active-author/failing-check items are kept for maintainer or author follow-up.

actions.reason: Related open issue for #81916; post-merge closeout is disabled and #81916 is not merge-ready in this result.

Small focused PR appears repairable, but unresolved review state blocks merge recommendation.
Broad i18n/generated-locale change with failing checks needs maintainer product and validation judgment; not a safe automated fix PR target.
Related context only; active author follow-up and failing/stale scope keep it out of this shard's finalization path.
Narrow compatibility repair is concrete, but branch refresh, review findings, and failing checks must be addressed before merge.
Already merged historical context for compaction work; no mutation is valid.
Concrete narrow repair artifact is safe to hand to executor for merge-prep.
Focused Doctor fix can be repaired and revalidated without replacing contributor work.
Narrow compatibility fix is a concrete repair candidate despite current failing check and waiting-on-author label.

needs_human: #81864 requires central OpenClaw security handling because it changes plugin approval boundary behavior.
